source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking a Forward Deployed Engineer to operate at the intersection of product, engineering, and the customer. You will be responsible for working shoulder to shoulder with military customers to design and deliver the end-to-end capabilities for real missions.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience in a technical role combining Software Engineering with customer-facing functions (Solutions Engineering, Sales Engineering, or Developer Relations) with a focus on Full-stack Development.

 - Proven experience engaging directly with end-users to understand mission needs, conduct valuable user testing, and incorporate product feedback.

 - Exceptional communication, collaboration, and interpersonal skills, with strong technical knowledge in distributed systems, autonomy platforms, data pipelines, and edge software.

 - Demonstrated ability to integrate with complex government software systems, including data platforms, Identity and Access Management (IdAM) providers, Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems, and Cross Domain Solutions (CDSs).




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Active U.S. Secret or Top Secret security clearance.

 - Experience in Golang, Python, and/or React development.

 - Experience with hybrid operating environments (cloud, on-prem, edge) and common Communication APIs (REST, gRPC, GraphQL, MQTT).

 - Background in DOD RMF, FedRAMP, or NIST 800-53 compliance.

 - Experience with low-latency FMV/video systems, tactical data standards (CoT, STANAG), and/or common command-and-control platforms (TAK, Maven, Lattice).

 - Expertise in DDIL environments, Disconnected DevOps (Docker/K3s, mesh networking), and/or multi-sensor fusion.

 - Demonstrated ability to build or invent (e.g., app, startup) and experience/interest in the defense industry.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: 120,000 - $200,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package at Picogrid.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance provided (if applicable)

 - Unlimited PTO (two-week minimum) and 11 paid holidays per year

 - Paid parental leave for both parents

 - Lunch provided when working in-office and a fully stocked kitchenette

 - Free EV charging at the HQ

 - Unique office in El Segundo, CA stocked with quality coffee, snacks, and craft beer





Export Control Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Policy

Picogrid is committed to providing a professional work environment free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. We are an equal opportunity employer and make all employment decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs.

source_jobs.title: Head of Hardware
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Head of Hardware at Picogrid, you will be the foundational technical leader responsible for defining, building, and scaling our entire hardware engineering function. You will lead the "cradle to grave" development of Picogrid’s physical products, with a hard focus on generalist hardware engineering, end-to-end design ownership across all disciplines, industrial design, and delivering best-in-class consumer product experiences. This is a highly demanding, hands-on role for a true generalist and leader ready to stand up a world-class department from the ground up. Work with a team of hardworking, ambitious, world-leading talent to build the future of unmanned system behavior orchestration.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the comprehensive hardware development lifecycle from initial concept and architecture through mass production.

 - Architect and execute all technical aspects of electrical and embedded systems, including circuit design, PCB design and layout, component selection, and system integration.

 - Architect and execute all technical aspects of mechanical and industrial design, including CAD, FEA, structural analysis, thermal analysis, DFMA, and GD&T.

 - Participate in defining engineering process standards, part numbering and revision systems, vendor relationships, R&D of supply chain, and design philosophy.

 - Define and implement the necessary design tools and methodologies for embedded design, ensuring a modern and efficient generalist hardware skillset across the team.

 - Act as the primary interface between Engineering, and Manufacturing, ensuring a product-minded approach that balances technical requirements with user experience and aesthetic quality.

 - Set the technical bar and drive a culture of being fast, flexible, and prolific, rapidly iterating on prototypes and moving from concept to high-volume production at record speed.

 - Embody and teach how to use intuition, judgement, and domain knowledge to speed up technical decisions and the design process.

 - You will be the founding member of the Hardware Engineering Department, tasked with hiring, mentoring, and leading a high-performing team of hardware engineers.

 - Successfully stand up a fully operational Hardware Engineering Department, deliver the next generation of our consumer-focused physical products, and establish the technical infrastructure for long-term product success.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of progressive experience in hardware product development, with significant experience in a hands-on technical lead or director-level role.

 - Preferred past founding or early stage fast-growth startup experience.

 - Ability to work with ambiguity in the managed chaos of a rapidly growing startup.

 - Expert-level proficiency in the full generalist hardware skillset, encompassing PCB design, power electronics, mixed-signal circuit design, firmware integration, CAD, mechanical/thermal engineering, industrial design, and manufacturing.

 - Proven experience in taking at least two distinct products through the entire development lifecycle, from initial concept to high-volume manufacturing.

 - Deep knowledge of DFMA (Design for Manufacturability and Assembly) and supply chain management for consumer electronics.

 - Demonstrated expertise with Altium and Onshape software suites. Familiarity with Arena PLM.

 - Passion for designing beautiful products.

 - Exceptional leadership, communication, and collaboration skills, with a clear ability to articulate technical strategy to

source_jobs.title: Fullstack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for entrepreneurial, product- and customer-focused full-stack engineers to own up to several bespoke UIs for users from interceptor drone navigators to security forces operators. When a feature needs changes in other parts of our system you will often jump in and help implement those. This is a high-impact, high-autonomy role where you’ll shape real-time geospatial UIs that integrate live video, maps, and controls for complex defense systems. You will work closely with product, design, and customers to turn ambiguous needs into shipping products at lightning speed. You will occasionally travel to the customer to help learn the real requirements and constraints and iterate with them in the field. The role is frontend-heavy (React / TypeScript), but you should be comfortable working across the stack when needed.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience building production frontend applications with React and TypeScript (or equivalent modern web stack).

 - Strong intuition for UI/UX.

 - Comfortable operating in ambiguity, taking fuzzy problems and driving toward concrete solutions with minimal high-leverage scope.

 - Strong ownership mindset focused on shipping customer value and delivering robust, usable solutions.

 - Full-stack or generalist experience, comfortable contributing to the backend when needed.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience optimizing web applications in resource and bandwidth constrained environments

 - Experience with real-time, data-heavy, or geospatial applications (e.g., maps, tracking, control systems, fleet management).

 - Experience with Deck.gl http://Deck.gl, Mapbox/MapLibre

 - High-agency, go-getter who enjoys high-ownership roles and shaping product/architecture in a fast-moving startup environment.

 - Collaborative and motivated by working on mission-critical, real-world problems.

 - Experience building ATAK integrations or other Android development.

 - Experience building offline-first web applications

 


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $150,000 - $220,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package at Picogrid.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance provided

 - Unlimited PTO (two-week minimum) and 11 paid holidays per year

 - Paid parental leave for both parents

 - Lunch provided when working in-office and fully stocked kitchenette

 - Remote/hybrid-friendly work environment

 - Free EV charging at the HQ

 - Unique office in El Segundo, CA stocked with quality coffee, snacks, and craft beer



#LI-DNP

Export Control Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Policy

Picogrid is committed to providing a professional work environment free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. We are an equal opportunity employer and make all employment decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs.

source_jobs.title: Growth Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Growth Strategist at Picogrid, you will drive the company’s growth across the U.S. Department of War and broader national security ecosystem. Sitting at the intersection of strategy, capture, customer engagement, and technical storytelling, you will identify emerging mission needs, shape opportunities, and translate complex technologies into compelling operational value for customers and partners.

You will work closely with company leadership, engineers, partners, customers, and the broader Growth team to build go-to-market strategies, lead capture efforts, and deliver high-impact communications–including proposals, white papers, presentations, and strategic briefs. If you thrive at the intersection of research, strategy, and written and verbal storytelling, you'll love this role.

The role is ideal for candidates who thrive in fast-paced environments, enjoy solving ambiguous problems, and are motivated to advance cutting-edge defense capabilities into real-world operations. Picogrid’s system integration technology supports a wide range of warfighting missions, creating an exceptionally broad addressable market. A key part of this role will involve helping the company prioritize opportunities, focus capture efforts, and allocate resources toward the highest-leverage missions and programs.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Conduct market intelligence and strategic analysis, including assessment of customer priorities, defense budget trends, and partnership opportunities.

 - Identify, qualify, and shape strategic opportunities across the Department of War, Intelligence Community, and other U.S. Government and partner nation customers.

 - Develop and execute capture strategies, including stakeholder engagement plans, teaming approaches, pricing strategies, and win themes.

 - Represent Picogrid at industry days, conferences, and pitches to strengthen customer relationships and expand partnerships.

 - Build compelling presentations and customer-facing materials, including executive briefings, read-ahead decks, and opportunity-specific pitch materials.

 - Lead the development of high-quality proposals, white papers, and other written communications.

 - Help build and scale Picogrid’s growth and proposal infrastructure, incorporating best practices from leading defense technology companies and the commercial sector.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - A Bachelor's Degree in a technical, analytical, policy, or business-related field.

 - 3+ years of experience in defense growth, capture, technical communications, think tank, management consulting, or similar fields.

 - Strong analytical and communications skills, including the ability to synthesize complex technical and operational concepts into compelling written and verbal narratives.

 - Demonstrated ability to operate independently in fast-paced, ambiguous environments with high ownership and accountability.

 - Familiarity with the U.S. Department of War, defense acquisition ecosystem, or national security technology landscape.

 - Experience developing customer-facing materials such as proposals, presentations, strategic briefs, or executive communications.

 - Ability to travel (<50%) to customer locations, including Washington, DC; Huntsville, AL; Colorado Springs, CO; and Tampa, FL.

 - Active or eligibility to obtain a US Government Security Clearance, noting that such clearances are only granted to US citizens.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience supporting or leading capture efforts and proposals for defense pro

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking a Senior Software Engineer to join our Legion Platform team. You will design, build, and deploy high-performance, scalable backend systems and integration tools that connect hardware and software systems across all environments and command layers. On the Legion team, your expertise will be critical for solving complex, system-level challenges and advancing a robust platform that is deployed for real-world military missions. This role requires constant innovation and suits an engineer who thrives under pressure and demands their work have immediate, tangible impact on national security.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of hands-on backend engineering experience in fast-paced, production environments using Go, Python, Rust, C/C++, TypeScript, or similar languages.

 - Strong background in APIs, distributed systems, data pipelines, or real-time processing.

 - Deep experience with relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL), object stores (e.g., S3, MinIO), and geospatial tools.

 - Strong grasp of HTTP APIs (REST, gRPC, GraphQL) and authentication/authorization models.

 - History of working directly with customers to build and deliver meaningful software.

 - Demonstrated ability to operate with urgency, ownership, and high engineering standards.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - U.S. citizenship and ability to obtain and maintain a Top Secret security clearance.

 - Exposure to DOD IL5+, Top Secret, and JWICS environments, including understanding of DOD RMF, FedRAMP, or NIST 800-53.

 - Experience supporting Golang-based applications in production.

 - Experience building plugins or integrations for common military command-and-control (C2) platforms (e.g. TAK, Maven, Lattice).




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $150,000 - $220,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package at Picogrid.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance provided (if applicable)

 - Unlimited PTO (two-week minimum) and 11 paid holidays per year

 - Paid parental leave for both parents

 - Lunch provided when working in-office and a fully stocked kitchenette

 - Free EV charging at the HQ

 - Unique office in El Segundo, CA stocked with quality coffee, snacks, and craft beer





Export Control Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.



Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Policy

Picogrid is committed to providing a professional work environment free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. We are an equal opportunity employer and make all employment decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs.

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Technical Recruiter to lead engineering hiring. You’ll own the full engineering recruiting funnel—from sourcing and outreach to closing—while building the internal systems that will scale our technical organization. This role sits at the intersection of technical recruiting and people operations, and will be foundational to how Picogrid’s engineering team grows.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own full-cycle recruiting for technical and operational roles (hardware, software, systems, and field).

 - Partner with leadership to define hiring needs and build role profiles.

 - Design and optimize recruiting processes from sourcing to interviews to offers.

 - Source, engage, and close high-caliber candidates across multiple disciplines.

 - Build and manage Picogrid’s talent pipeline for critical roles.

 - Represent Picogrid’s mission and culture to every candidate interaction.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience, with at least 3 years focused on software and hardware technical recruiting.

 - Proven ability to source and close top technical talent in competitive markets.

 - Experience recruiting for early-stage startups, defense, aerospace, or frontier technology is a strong plus.

 - Excellent communicator and relationship builder both internally and externally.

 - Highly organized and data-driven; you track metrics and iterate systems.

 - Mission-oriented: you want to solve hard problems that matter to national defense.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Proven ability to thrive in resource-constrained, high-ambiguity, and fast-paced environments.

 - Familiarity with the defense technology landscape, including an understanding of procurement cycles, ITAR/EAR compliance, and the specific challenges of mission-critical systems.

 - Hands-on experience designing and optimizing full-cycle recruiting processes, including ATS configuration and implementing reporting metrics.

 - Ability to understand complex technical requirements for roles like Backend SWEs, DevOps, and EEs, and translate them into effective sourcing strategies.

 - Experience leveraging talent data (e.g., market trends, diversity metrics) to guide sourcing strategy and influence hiring manager decision-making.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $120,000- $150,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package at Picogrid.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance provided (if applicable)

 - Unlimited PTO (two-week minimum) and 11 paid holidays per year

 - Paid parental leave for both parents

 - Lunch provided when working in-office and a fully stocked kitchenette

 - Free EV charging at the HQ

 - Unique office in El Segundo, CA stocked with quality coffee, snacks, and craft beer

 
 

#LI-DNP

source_jobs.title: Head of Software
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Head of Software at Picogrid, you will be the foundational people leader responsible for scaling our software engineering team. This is a highly demanding, hands-on role for a true generalist and leader ready to grow a world-class software department.

You will set the team structure and hiring goals across teams that handle backend platforms, real-time geospatial UIs, hardware-software integration, and operator-facing command-and-control systems. You won't just manage, you will architect, build, and ship alongside your team. Work with hardworking, ambitious, world-leading talent to build the future of unmanned system behavior orchestration.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the team's growth, people management, and strategic planning across all layers of the Picogrid software stack.

 - Collaborate with the CTO, the Lead Software Engineer, Product Owners, and Engineering Managers to produce plans and strategy to handle the product and customer development goals.

 - Act as the primary interface between Software Engineering and all other functions (Go-To-Market, Hardware, and Operations) ensuring software delivers real operational impact for users in the field.

 - Represent the company in external conversations with strategic partners and customers.

 - Participate in yearly, quarterly, and monthly planning cycles. Contribute to the development of the product roadmap.

 - As the founding leader of the Software Engineering Department, you will hire, mentor, and build a high-performing team of engineers across backend, frontend, and platform disciplines.

 - Successfully grow a fully operational Software Engineering Department, deliver the next generation of our platform and operator products, and establish the technical infrastructure for long-term mission success.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of progressive software engineering experience, with significant time in a hands-on technical lead, principal, or director-level role.

 - Preferred: past founding or early-stage fast-growth startup experience.

 - Strong inter-personal skills and demonstrated ability to grow people.

 - Ability to operate with clarity and urgency in the managed chaos of a rapidly growing startup.

 - Expert-level proficiency across the full generalist software skillset, spanning backend systems, event-based architecture, distributed architecture, APIs (REST, gRPC, GraphQL), real-time data pipelines, and production operations.

 - Deep familiarity with modern languages and runtimes used in production: Go, Python, and TypeScript.

 - Strong grasp of relational databases (PostgreSQL), object storage, geospatial tooling, and high-reliability deployment patterns.

 - Meaningful experience leading both backend platform work and frontend/UI development for complex, data-intensive applications.

 - Proven experience taking multiple distinct products through the full development lifecycle, from initial architecture to production at scale.

 - Exceptional leadership, communication, and collaboration skills, with a clear ability to translate technical strategy into product outcomes and articulate it to executive leadership.

 - Core values of relentless execution, boundless ambition, continued learning, and kindness. Low ego and open-mindedness.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience as a former founder or founding engineer at a successful, fast-moving software company, with a track record of standing up a team or discipline from zero.

 - U.S. citizenship and ability to obtain and maintain a T

source_jobs.title: Field Operations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking a Field Operations Engineer to help build the next generation of American defense technology. As a core member of Picogrid’s engineering team, you will help define our engineering culture and product roadmap.

As a Field Operations Engineer, you will work on the PCBAs and electrical systems powering Picogrid's current (e.g. Lander http://picogrid.com/lander, Helios http://picogrid.com/helios) and future (unannounced) hardware platforms, enabling the deployment and operation of unmanned and autonomous systems into operations around the world.

You will own the fleet maintenance processes from blank-sheet design to in-field troubleshooting and work alongside Picogrid’s world-class engineering team coming from companies including SpaceX, Google, Twitter, and more.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Travel to unique parts of the country. Work with cutting edge technology in a fast paced, highly iterative engineering culture. See your work's impact firsthand as you create value for customers in every project.

 - Configure network equipment, flash circuit boards, configure Linux machines by running bash scripts in R&D and field settings.

 - Travel 50-70% of your time across interesting and unique parts of the country to perform equipment installations and servicing.

 - Write python and bash scripts to automate configuration processes.

 - Collaborate with Electrical, Embedded and Software Engineers during design stages.

 - Prepare and maintain detailed design documentation for your work.

 - Contribute to the preparation of technical reports and presentations for stakeholders.

 - Support prototype development and perform hands-on testing to validate design performance.

 - Obsess about quality, look for improvements that speed up and simplify processes.

 - Develop jigs and stations to increase speed and yield of manufacturing.

 - Respect workplace safety guidelines and take LV DC safety seriously.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - No degree required. Preferred expertise related to Network Engineering, IT, Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Computer Science or a related field.

 - Growth mindset, desire to learn, results-oriented focus.

 - Proven experience in network equipment configuration.

 - Proficiency in network equipment configuration.

 - Proficiency in harnessing assembly and installation.

 - Proficiency in interpreting and applying design specifications, datasheets, and technical drawings.

 - Familiarity with Linux.

 - Familiarity with command line interface, bash.

 - Proficiency in leveraging modern tools to 10x your productivity.

 - Strong knowledge of networks, electrical, and harnessing systems.

 - Experience with testing tools such as multimeters, oscilloscopes, DAQs, etc.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 4+ years of experience with IT, electrical/mechanical assembly

 - Military, aerospace, or IC background

 - Track record of leadership demonstrated through management, public service, community organizing, or similar experience




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $70,000 - $120,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package at Picogrid.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance provided (if applicable)

 - Unlimited PTO (two-week minimum) and 11 paid holidays per year

 - Paid parental leave for both parents

 - Lunch pr

source_jobs.title: Senior Deployment Strategist - Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Deployment Strategist, Partnerships at Picogrid, you will operate as a senior member of our deployments team, responsible for driving successful deployments of Picogrid’s platform in complex, real-world environments.

Senior Deployment Strategists at Picogrid are responsible for working directly with customers to implement our technology, ensuring it delivers operational value, and driving execution across engineering, product, and external stakeholders. This requires strong technical fluency, sound judgment in ambiguous environments, and the ability to take ownership of outcomes in the field.

In addition, this role includes a focused responsibility for building and scaling Picogrid’s partnerships capability.

Picogrid’s platform depends on its ability to connect and operationalize a growing ecosystem of partner technologies. You will ensure that partner systems are effectively integrated into deployments and that partner relationships translate into real operational outcomes.

You will also help institutionalize how Picogrid manages partnerships, building the processes and systems needed to coordinate partner relationships across technical employment, proposal efforts, communications, and public-facing engagements.

This is a role for someone who can both execute as a Senior Deployment Strategist in the field and build the structure that allows partner-enabled deployments to scale across the company.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Deployment Execution
   
   - Own execution of partner-enabled deployments, ensuring integrated systems perform reliably in real-world environments
   
   - Work alongside other Deployment Strategists to plan and execute deployments involving multiple partner systems
   
   - Ensure partner technologies are deployment-ready, interoperable, and aligned with mission requirements
   
   - Serve as a primary interface between Picogrid, partners, and customers during deployments, exercises, and pilots
   
   - Identify integration gaps and drive coordination with Engineering and Product teams to resolve them

 - Partnerships Program Expansion
   
   - Build and improve systems to scale Picogrid’s partnerships program across deployments and programs
   
   - Coordinate and track partner relationships across technical integrations, proposal efforts, communications, and public-facing engagements
   
   - Develop processes that ensure consistent partner engagement and alignment across internal teams
   
   - Maintain visibility into partner activity, performance, and strategic alignment
   
   - Support partner involvement in proposals, pilots, and deployments, ensuring coordination across teams
   
   - Help identify and onboard new partners aligned with Picogrid’s platform and mission




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have deep experience operating in deployment environments, defense programs, or similarly demanding operational settings

 - You know how to leverage technology in the field and drive outcomes in environments where requirements shift and ambiguity is the default

 - You can quickly build working knowledge of new technical domains and operate effectively across the diverse systems Picogrid connects

 - You are comfortable working across customers, partners, and technical teams to make integrated efforts succeed

 - You can build structure where needed, but you never lose sight of execution and mission outcomes

 - You think in terms of systems, interoperability, and operational impact, not just tasks or meetings

 - You are collaborative, low-eg

source_jobs.title: General Counsel
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking a General Counsel to lead all legal and compliance functions as we scale our technology into active operations around the world. You will be the company's first dedicated legal hire, reporting directly to a co-founder and sitting at the table for every significant decision: government contracts, commercial deals, export compliance, fundraising, and corporate governance.

The right candidate has navigated the complexity of defense contracting, built or managed an export compliance program, and knows how to give clear guidance in ambiguous situations without slowing the business down. You're passionate about the law and policy of defense technology, and have an independent interest in the issues at the frontier of this field.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as the company's primary legal advisor across all functions: government contracts, commercial agreements, corporate governance, employment, IP, and regulatory compliance.

 - Own Picogrid's export compliance program, including ITAR and EAR, ensuring Picogrid stays ahead of a rapidly evolving regulatory landscape.

 - Assist government contracts across the full procurement lifecycle, from proposal review and contract award through performance and closeout, with fluency in FAR/DFARS, OTAs, and SBIRs.

 - Partner with the go-to-market and program teams to support proposal strategy, contract structuring, and customer negotiations.

 - Support fundraising and strategic transactions alongside finance and leadership.

 - Manage outside counsel relationships effectively, scoping work, controlling cost, and maintaining quality.

 - Monitor regulatory and policy developments relevant to Picogrid's business, including ITAR/EAR revisions, NDAA provisions, and acquisition reform, and translate implications for the leadership team.

 - Advise on employment matters and sensitive HR situations as they arise.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - J.D. from an accredited law school; active bar membership in at least one U.S. state.

 - 8+ years of legal experience, with significant time in-house at a defense technology, aerospace, or government contracting company.

 - Prior experience as General Counsel or senior counsel with broad ownership of a company's legal portfolio.

 - Hands-on experience with ITAR and EAR, including classification analysis, license applications, and compliance program management.

 - Substantive government contracts experience, including FAR/DFARS, contract vehicles, and data rights.

 - Judgment and initiative to own complex matters end-to-end with limited resources and no legal team behind you.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience building legal and compliance infrastructure at a venture-backed company.

 - Familiarity with CMMC, CUI handling requirements, and information security frameworks relevant to DoW contractors.

 - Background supporting Series A+ fundraising and M&A diligence.

 - Active security clearance, or eligibility and willingness to obtain one.

 - Experience with non-traditional contract vehicles, including OTAs and SBIRs.

 - A genuine interest in defense technology and the mission it supports.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $190,000 - $250,000 per year plus performance incentive. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance pro

source_jobs.title: Head of Government Affairs
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As Picogrid's first Head of Government Affairs, you will own the company's entire relationship with Washington: from Capitol Hill to the Pentagon. You will build and execute a government affairs strategy that accelerates Picogrid's access to defense programs, shapes policy in the unmanned systems and C2 space, and ensures policymakers understand what is at stake as autonomous systems become central to modern warfare.

You will build the in-house function from zero to one, establish Picogrid's presence in DC, and serve as the primary voice of the company to the U.S. government. You will work directly with company leadership to translate our operational track record and technology into policy wins, procurement opportunities, and strategic relationships.

The right person for this role is equally comfortable in a congressional hearing room and a field briefing. You understand defense acquisition as well as you understand appropriations. You can distill how a hardware-software stack enabling unmanned system orchestration changes the character of warfare, and explain why that matters to a staffer with five minutes to spare.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Own and execute Picogrid's full government affairs strategy at the federal level: build and maintain deep relationships with key decision-makers across Congress, OSD, the military services, combatant commands, and relevant interagency stakeholders, including defense appropriations and armed services committees.

 - Drive Picogrid's positioning in relevant defense budget cycles: identifying funding lines, influencing program office priorities, and supporting business development with strategic intelligence from the Hill and Pentagon.

 - Develop and communicate clear, compelling policy positions on issues critical to Picogrid's mission, including export controls, open architecture standards, and technology competition with peer adversaries.

 - Serve as a strategic advisor to the CEO and executive team on political developments, policy risks, and government relationship management, and represent Picogrid externally at key industry events, hearings, conferences, and stakeholder engagements.

 - Hire and manage an internal team, external advisors, lobbyists, and consultants as the function grows.

 - Build coalitions with aligned defense tech companies, trade associations, and national security organizations to advance shared policy goals.

 


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of experience in government affairs, defense policy, congressional affairs, or related roles, with a demonstrable track record of influencing policy and procurement outcomes.

 - Experience building out a Government Affairs function and team at a high growth technology company

 - Demonstrated ability to translate complex technical capabilities into accessible, compelling policy narratives.

 - Comfort operating as a founder-minded, high-ownership individual contributor

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills; executive presence and credibility with senior government officials.

 - Willingness to travel regularly between El Segundo and Washington, DC, and to attend field and customer engagements as needed.

 


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Direct experience in or with DoD, a military service, or the Office of the Secretary of Defense, particularly in acquisition, requirements, or technology policy.

 - Familiarity with the unmanned systems, autonomous systems, or C2/ISR policy landscape, including relevant programs and offices (e.g., CDAO, OUSD R&E, DIU

source_jobs.title: Harnessing Technician
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking a Harnessing Technician to help build the next generation of American defense technology. As a core member of Picogrid’s hardware engineering team, you will help define our engineering culture and product roadmap.

As a Harnessing Technician, you will work on the PCBAs and electrical systems powering Picogrid's current (e.g. Lander http://picogrid.com/lander, Helios http://picogrid.com/helios) and future (unannounced) hardware platforms, enabling the deployment of unmanned and autonomous systems into operations around the world.

You will own the wire harness manufacturing process from blank-sheet design to in-field troubleshooting and work alongside Picogrid’s world-class engineering team coming from companies including SpaceX, Google, Twitter, and more.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Assume ownership over the harness manufacturing process, working with the rest of the engineering team in assembling, maintaining, and operating mechanical and electrical systems

 - Obsess about quality, look for improvements that speed up and simplify processes

 - Perform a variety of mechanical and wiring tasks, including repetitive and non-repetitive fabrication, final end product assembly, and field maintenance

 - Develop manufacturing jigs to increase speed and yield of harness manufacturing

 - Follow electrical schematics and harness drawings, and complete wiring tasks including crimping, building harnesses, wiring switchgear, soldering SMT and through-hole components

 - Troubleshoot and rework circuit designs in development

 - Prepare and execute electrical and environmental test plans for PCBAs and end products

 - Manage part inventory for harness and PCBA assembly processes

 - Organize and maintain tooling and equipment involved in harness manufacturing

 - Respect workplace safety guidelines and take LV DC safety seriously

 - Travel 15-25% of your time across interesting and unique parts of the country to perform equipment installations and servicing




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years of experience with electrical/mechanical assembly

 - Associate degree

 - Military, aerospace, or IC background

 - Track record of leadership demonstrated through management, public service, community organizing, or similar experience




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $70,000 - $120,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package at Picogrid.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance provided (if applicable)

 - Unlimited PTO (two-week minimum) and 11 paid holidays per year

 - Paid parental leave for both parents

 - Lunch provided when working in-office and a fully stocked kitchenette

 - Free EV charging at the HQ

 - Unique office in El Segundo, CA stocked with quality coffee, snacks, and craft beer





Export Control Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.





#LI-DNP

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Policy

Picogrid is committed to providing a professional work environment free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. We are an equal opportunity e

Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking an Assembly Technician to help build the next generation of American defense technology. As a core member of Picogrid’s hardware engineering team, you will help define our engineering culture and product roadmap.

As a Assembly Technician, you will work on the enclosures and structural systems comprising Picogrid's current and future hardware platforms https://picogrid.com/edge-nodes, enabling the deployment of unmanned and autonomous systems into operations around the world.

You will own the mechanical assembly process, from design documentation and work instructions to in-field troubleshooting. You will work collaboratively alongside Picogrid’s world-class engineering team coming from leading technology companies including SpaceX, Google, Twitter, and more.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Assume ownership over the mechanical assembly process, working with the rest of the engineering team in assembling, maintaining, and operating mechanical and electrical systems.

 - Respect workplace safety guidelines and take LV DC safety seriously.

 - Free EV charging at the HQ

 - Unique office in El Segundo, CA stocked with quality coffee, snacks, and craft beer

source_jobs.title: Senior Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking a Senior Electrical Engineer to design, build, and deploy mission-critical hardware systems that support U.S. defense operations. This is a hands-on role at the intersection of embedded systems, mechanical integration, and high-reliability design, where you will own key electrical components that power connected autonomous systems in the field.

You will own the full design lifecycle for various hardware products: from rapid prototyping and validation through production and direct field deployment. Your work is not bench-bound; you will collaborate closely with software, mechanical, and deployment teams to ensure systems operate reliably in harsh environments from deserts to flightlines to active field operations. Expect fast iteration and minimal layers between your engineering and the end user, driving real-world mission impact.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of industry experience designing and releasing custom PCBAs into production.

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering.

 - Deep understanding of high-speed digital design, signal integrity, power electronics, and common electronic components. Proven experience in schematic capture and PCB design for high-reliability applications (aerospace, defense, or similar industries preferred).

 - Proficiency in PCB design software (KiCAD or Altium preferred), circuit simulation (LTSpice preferred), and testing tools (oscilloscopes, DMM, bench power supplies, data loggers).

 - Expertise in power electronics design, PCIE, USB-C 3.2, Ethernet, and POE.

 - Growth mindset, results-oriented focus, and proficiency in leveraging modern tools to maximize productivity.

 - Ability to interpret and apply design specifications, datasheets, and technical drawings.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Familiarity with Embedded Engineering, STM32, FreeRTOS, C/C++, Rust.

 - Familiarity with git and CAD (OnShape preferred).

 - Experience with defense, aerospace, or other high-reliability hardware, and designing systems for environmental robustness (temperature, vibration, ingress protection).

 - Comfort supporting end-to-end system deployment, including in field settings.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $180,000 - $220,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation package at Picogrid.

 - Significant stock options with a high potential upside as an early-stage company

 - 401(k) with employer matching

 - Full health coverage (medical, dental, and vision insurance)

 - Relocation assistance provided (if applicable)

 - Unlimited PTO (two-week minimum) and 11 paid holidays per year

 - Paid parental leave for both parents

 - Lunch provided when working in-office and a fully stocked kitchenette

 - Free EV charging at the HQ

 - Unique office in El Segundo, CA stocked with quality coffee, snacks, and craft beer





Export Control Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Policy

Picogrid is committed to providing a professional work environment free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. We are an equal opportunity employer and make all employment decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs.

source_jobs.title: Site Lead
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is opening a manufacturing facility at FISTA Innovation Park in Lawton, Oklahoma, adjacent to Fort Sill. This site will support production for Picogrid’s core product lines. We are looking for a Site Lead to build, run, and grow our Lawton presence from the ground up.

This role sits at the intersection of operations, community engagement, and business development. You will be the senior Picogrid representative on the ground in Lawton, responsible for standing up and managing day-to-day office operations, cultivating relationships with Fort Sill leadership and local government stakeholders, and identifying opportunities to expand Picogrid’s footprint across the region. You will work closely with our founders, engineering team, and business development organization to ensure the Lawton site operates as a high-performing extension of Picogrid’s headquarters in El Segundo.

This is a role for someone who is equal parts operator, relationship builder, and strategic thinker. You should be comfortable managing office operations in the morning, meeting with a garrison commander in the afternoon, and reviewing a proposal opportunity in the evening. The right candidate will see this as a chance to build something meaningful in a community that is rapidly becoming a hub for defense innovation.

At Picogrid, we look for people who are scrappy, ambitious, and thrive in high-intensity environments. We move fast, solve hard problems, and push boundaries in defense technology. Above all, we act with integrity toward our team, our customers, and the mission.





REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 10+ years of professional experience in military service, government relations, or a combination of these areas.

 - Demonstrated ability to establish, prioritize, nurture, and grow relationships with Department of War partners and industry executive leadership with a high degree of independence.

 - Experience engaging with U.S. Army leadership, installation management, or local government stakeholders in a professional capacity.

 - Strong operational instincts with the ability to manage logistics and personnel in a fast-moving environment.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical capabilities into mission-relevant language for diverse audiences.

 - Ability to thrive in an autonomous, fast-paced, and demanding startup environment with minimal oversight.

 - Self-starter mentality with a bias for action & ownership of deliverables.

 - Low ego, focused on team success, delivering impactful outcomes, and passionately solving mission challenges for our partners.

 - Active or eligibility to obtain a U.S. Government security clearance.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Prior service or deep familiarity with Fort Sill, the Fires Center of Excellence, or Army training and doctrine organizations.

 - Existing relationships within the Oklahoma defense and political community, including state legislature, economic development agencies, and installation leadership.

 - Knowledge of unmanned systems, C5ISR, sensor integration, or related technologies for ground-based and multi-domain operations.

 - Experience working with or scaling an early-stage defense technology company into a growth-stage enterprise.

 - Familiarity with federal and state incentive programs for defense manufacturing and economic development.




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Base salary range: $105,000- $175,000 per year. Base salary is just one part of your total compensation pa

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - Ukraine
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Picogrid is seeking a Senior Software Engineer to join our Legion Platform team. You will design, build, and deploy high-performance, scalable backend systems and integration tools that connect hardware and software systems across all environments and command layers. On the Legion team, your expertise will be critical for solving complex, system-level challenges and advancing a robust platform that is deployed for real-world military missions. This role requires constant innovation and suits an engineer who thrives under pressure and demands their work have an immediate, tangible impact on national security. This position is based at our office in Kyiv, Ukraine.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of hands-on backend engineering experience in fast-paced, production environments using either Go, Python, Rust, C/C++, TypeScript, or similar languages.

 - Strong background in APIs, distributed systems, data pipelines, or real-time processing.

 - Deep experience with relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL), object stores (e.g., S3, MinIO), and geospatial tools.

 - Strong grasp of HTTP APIs (REST, gRPC or GraphQL) and authentication/authorization models.

 - History of working directly with customers to build and deliver meaningful software.

 - Demonstrated ability to operate with urgency, ownership, and high engineering standards.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience working with Ukrainian Ministry of Defence, and/or NATO members.

 - Exposure to Ukrainian military C2 platforms, including Delta, Kretchet, Oreanda, etc.

 - Experience supporting Golang-based applications in production.

 - Experience building plugins or integrations for common military command-and-control (C2) platforms (e.g. TAK, Maven, Lattice).




COMPENSATION

 - Salary range: $5,000-$9,500 USD monthly wages, depending on experience

 - Meaningful stock options

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Policy

Picogrid is committed to providing a professional work environment free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. We are an equal opportunity employer and make all employment decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs.

source_jobs.title: Senior Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

Picogrid is a leading venture-backed defense technology company founded to bridge the decades-long gap between modern technology and the critical demands of national security. Today, we're building the essential infrastructure to unify sensors, autonomy, and operators with our technology deployed in active operations around the world. Our mission is to deliver an operational advantage to secure the United States and its allies.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Deployment Strategist at Picogrid, you will be responsible for deploying and operationalizing Picogrid’s platform in complex, real-world environments.

You will work directly with customers—often in mission-critical settings—to ensure our technology delivers real operational value. This includes configuring systems, integrating diverse technologies, and adapting quickly as requirements evolve.

These deployments often involve multiple systems, stakeholders, and constraints, and success depends on your ability to understand the full system, make sound decisions in ambiguous environments, and drive execution end-to-end.

You will also play a critical role internally—translating field needs into product improvements, informing engineering priorities, and shaping how Picogrid evolves its platform and deployments.

This is a role for someone who wants to own outcomes in the field, operate across technical domains, and work at the intersection of product, customer, and mission.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own end-to-end execution of deployments, programs, and customer engagements, ensuring Picogrid delivers meaningful operational outcomes

 - Drive project planning, prioritization, and execution across multiple workstreams, stakeholders, and timelines

 - Lead coordination across customers, partners, Government Solutions, and Engineering teams to ensure alignment and progress

 - Engage directly with senior military leadership and partner company executives, representing Picogrid in high-stakes operational, technical, and strategic discussions

 - Design and execute exercises, demonstrations, and test environments that validate capabilities and inform product development

 - Translate customer needs into clear execution plans and technical requirements, and ensure follow-through to delivery

 - Identify risks, dependencies, and blockers across efforts and drive them to resolution

 - Support execution of contracts, pilots, and operational programs, ensuring commitments are met and expectations are exceeded

 - Operate as a trusted partner to customers, maintaining strong relationships and ensuring Picogrid delivers consistent value

 - Provide leadership within Picogrid by driving clarity, setting priorities, and ensuring teams stay aligned on high-impact work

 - Capture insights from deployments and customer interactions to influence product direction, partnerships, and strategy




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have experience operating in real-world deployment environments or similarly ambiguous, high-stakes settings

 - You are comfortable taking ownership of outcomes—not just contributing to execution

 - You can quickly understand and apply high-level technical concepts across a wide range of systems and technologies

 - You think in terms of systems and outcomes, not just tasks

 - You are highly effective in fast-moving environments and can create clarity where none exists

 - You are collaborative and low-ego, and you build trust with customers, partners, and teammates

 - You have strong judgment and can prioritize effectively under pressure




BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years of experience in deployments, operations, defense partnerships, program execution, or similar roles

 - Demonstrated experience owning execution in cross-functional or customer-facing environments

 - Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences

 - Demonstrated ability to operate in ambiguous or high-pressure environments

 - Technical fluency—you can work effectively

notes: Defense technology company with strong engineering focus
